claim: PLAN-scenario-generated-spec.md §3 W2 (the obligatoriness wave) makes the scenario-generated-spec layer's obligations mechanically real rather than aspirational, and each of its four gates shall be anchored here as the claim it enforces: check_settled_requires_scenario (in a domain whose manifest declares discipline:"full", every SETTLED requirement carries a real carrier -- enforced_by OR implemented_by+verified_by+at-least-one-scenario-narrated verified_by test; an honest no-op for every domain that has not opted in); check_scenario_executes_impl (for every authored-path requirement with implemented_by AND verified_by both non-empty, regardless of discipline:full, every implemented_by symbol is actually executed by at least one verified_by test under real `go test -coverprofile` instrumentation); check_spec_md_current (a committed docs/gen/SPEC.md, when present, is byte-identical to a fresh `hotam gen-spec --spec` run; an honest no-op for a domain with no SPEC.md yet); and check_model_complete (in a discipline:"full" domain, every authored model object with at least one exported method cited as implemented_by is COMPLETE, each cited method backed by a scenario-narrated verified_by test). Each gate is an honest no-op before its own opt-in trigger fires (no discipline:full, no SPEC.md, no authored-path pair), so the migration is per-domain and carries no long-lived opt-out flag.
why: PLAN-scenario-generated-spec.md §0/§2 D4-D5/§3 W2: the authored-spec discipline MUST be mechanically obligatory once a domain opts in, not held together by text. Before W2, the engine's authored-spec layer was a NO-OP contract (authored_links.go:33-36) -- a consumer domain with zero authored models passed every check cleanly, so the methodology connected into sub-projects as INSTRUCTION only, never as a binding obligation. W2's four gates close that: each turns one facet of the scenario-generated-spec layer (per-requirement carrier existence, coverage-proof of the carrier, generated SPEC.md currency, model-level completeness) into a mechanical gate with an honest opt-in trigger, so obligation is per-domain and one-way (the discipline:"full" flip lands in the same commit that completes a domain's migration, per §2 D4). These four check_* invariants are the engine mechanism that makes this requirement ENFORCED; the gates are registered in internal/invariants/ (scenario_discipline.go, scenario_coverage.go, spec_md_current.go, model_complete.go) and each has its own mutation-round-trip test proving it fires on a real mutated fixture and clears once fixed. This requirement is the self-hosting anchor (W3.2 spirit) for the four orphan-enforcer gates check_bijection_r_to_enforcer flagged on hotam-spec-self: the engine holds itself to the same obligation it imposes on consumer domains.
evidence: PLAN-scenario-generated-spec.md §2 D3-D5 and §3 W2.1-W2.4 name these four gates verbatim and the opt-in shape; internal/invariants/scenario_discipline.go, scenario_coverage.go, spec_md_current.go, model_complete.go register each check_* with its full Rule/Why; this anchor closes the W3.2 self-hosting debt where check_bijection_r_to_enforcer flagged all four as orphan enforcers on hotam-spec-self (the engine modeling itself must anchor its own gates to claims, exactly as consumer domains must)
